title: calceolaria polyrhiza
intro: Calceolaria polyrhiza, also known as marsh marigold or slough marsh marigold, is a perennial plant species that belongs to the calceolaria family. Traditionally, the plant is used in herbal medicine to treat various ailments, with its medicinal properties attributed to the presence of flavonoids, alkaloids, and saponins, which exhibit anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties. The health benefits of Calceolaria polyrhiza include its potential to reduce inflammation, improve respiratory health, and exhibit antiviral and antibacterial activities. Calceolaria polyrhiza can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and decoctions, which are often used to support immune function, reduce fever, and alleviate symptoms of colds and flu.
